After fixing the main file, read each test file above and check if any test:
- Opens a dropdown/menu/popover and queries for content (getByText, getByRole)
- Uses querySelector with OUIA selectors to interact with components
- Asserts on element roles, aria attributes, or data attributes that changed
If any test would break due to your changes, fix it. Common test fixes:
- Wrap assertions in waitFor() when content renders via portal
- Add popperProps={{ appendTo: 'inline' }} to component renders in tests
- Update getByRole/getByText queries to match new attribute values

Apply ALL of the following 7 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Lines: 460, 469
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: primary

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Rule [family:ExpandableSection]:
## ExpandableSection Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <ExpandableSection contentId={...} direction={...} displaySize={...} isDetached={...} isExpanded={...} isIndented={...} isWidthLimited={...} onToggle={...}>
    <ExpandableSectionToggle contentId={...} direction={...} hasTruncatedContent={...} isDetached={...} isExpanded={...} onToggle={...} toggleId={...}>
      <ExpandableSection />
    </ExpandableSectionToggle>
  </ExpandableSection>
```

Props that stay on <ExpandableSection>: contentId, direction, displaySize, isDetached, isExpanded, isIndented, isWidthLimited, onToggle, toggleContent, toggleId, toggleText, toggleTextCollapsed, toggleTextExpanded, truncateMaxLines, variant

Removed props (move to child component as children or remove):
  isActive -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)

### Fix 3
Lines: 457, 467
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: ActionGroup

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

```

  457              <Button
  458                data-test-id="create-button"

  (line 467):
  466              </Button>
  467              <Button
  468                data-test="cancel-button"

```

### Fix 4
Lines: 332, 348
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-helpertextitem]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <HelperTextItem> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <HelperTextItem> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <HelperTextItem variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <HelperTextItem> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <HelperTextItem><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</HelperTextItem>

Rule [semver-text-component-import-deprecated]:
MIGRATION: Replace <Text> with props on <Content>.

Property mapping:
  - Text.ouiaId  →  Content.ouiaId
  - Text.component  →  Content.component
  - Text.className  →  Content.className
  - Text.ouiaSafe  →  Content.ouiaSafe
  - Text.children  →  Content.children
  - Text.isVisitedLink  →  Content.isVisitedLink

Remove <Text> from JSX and move its props to <Content>.
Also remove Text from the import statement.

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/mco/components/create-dr-policy/create-dr-policy.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the source file to understand the current code.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1**: `ButtonVariant.primary` and `ButtonVariant.secondary` — the type just added `'stateful'` to the union. The values `primary` and `secondary` are still valid. No code change needed.

**Fix 2**: `ExpandableSection` signature changed from `React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>` to `Omit<React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>, 'onToggle'>`. The code uses `ExpandableSection` at line 111 with `toggleText` prop, no `onToggle`. The `isActive` prop was removed, but the code doesn't use it. No code change needed.

**Fix 3**: Icon-to-prop for Button. Looking at lines 457-473, the Buttons contain text children (`{t('Create')}` and `{t('Cancel')}`), not icons. No icon content to move. No change needed.

**Fix 4**: Icon-to-prop for HelperTextItem. Looking at lines 332 and 348, the HelperTextItems contain text content, not icons. No change needed.

**Fix 5**: `TextVariants` → `ContentVariants`. Used at line 33 (import) and line 304 (`TextVariants.small`).

**Fix 6**: `Text` → `Content`. Used at line 30 (import) and line 304 (`<Text component={TextVariants.small}>`).

**Fix 7**: `TextContent` → `Content`. Used at line 31 (import) and line 303 (`<TextContent ...>`).

Fixes 5, 6, and 7 are related — `Text` and `TextContent` both become `Content`, and `TextVariants` becomes `ContentVariants`. The JSX at lines 303-309:
```jsx
<TextContent className="mco-create-data-policy__description">
  <Text component={TextVariants.small}>
    {t('...')}
  </Text>
</TextContent>
```
Should become:
```jsx
<Content className="mco-create-data-policy__description" component={ContentVariants.small}>
  {t('...')}
</Content>
```

The `<TextContent>` wrapper and `<Text>` inner element merge into a single `<Content>` element, with the `component` and `className` props combined on it.

Now let me apply the changes:

arguments.after:
<Content className="mco-create-data-policy__description" component={ContentVariants.small}>
          {t(
            'Get a quick recovery in a remote or secondary cluster with a disaster recovery (DR) policy'
          )}
        </Content>

          <Text component={TextVariants.small}>
            {t(
              'Get a quick recovery in a remote or secondary cluster with a disaster recovery (DR) policy'
            )}
          </Text>
        </TextContent>

content.text: Good, the JSX changes look correct. Now let me read the companion test file:

The test file uses `describe.skip(...)` so all tests are skipped. The tests use `screen.getByText(...)` to query for the description text, and `screen.getByTestId(...)` for buttons. My changes:

1. **Text content**: The description text string is unchanged — just moved from inside `<Text>` to directly inside `<Content>`. `getByText('Get a quick recovery...')` will still find it. ✅
2. **Button elements**: No changes to Button structure, so `getByTestId('create-button')` and `getByTestId('policy-name')` still work. ✅
3. No dropdown/menu/popover interactions in these tests. ✅
4. No OUIA selectors or aria attribute queries affected. ✅

The test file does not need any changes.

Let me do a final verification of the complete modified source file:

The file looks correct. All changes have been applied properly. Let me verify the test file one more time to confirm no changes are needed:

The test file (`create-dr-policy.spec.tsx`) uses `describe.skip(...)` so all tests are skipped. Even if they ran, the changes I made (replacing `<TextContent>` + `<Text>` with `<Content>`) don't affect:
- `screen.getByText(...)` queries — the text content is unchanged
- `screen.getByTestId(...)` queries — no test IDs were modified
- No dropdown/menu/popover interactions exist in these tests
- No OUIA selectors or aria attribute queries are affected

No test file changes needed.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (added `'stateful'`)
**No change needed.** The existing values `ButtonVariant.primary` (line 457/460) and `ButtonVariant.secondary` (line 467/469) are still valid in the new union type. The change only *added* `'stateful'` as a new option.

### Fix 2 — `ExpandableSection` signature changed (`Omit<..., 'onToggle'>`)
**No change needed.** The component at line 110 uses `toggleText` but does not use `onToggle` or `isActive` (the removed prop). The signature change doesn't affect existing usage.

### Fix 3 — `Button` icon children to `icon` prop
**No change needed.** The two `<Button>` instances (lines 454-463 and 464-470) contain only text children (`{t('Create')}` and `{t('Cancel')}`), not icon elements. No icon-to-prop migration is applicable.

### Fix 4 — `HelperTextItem` icon children to `icon` prop
**No change needed.** The `<HelperTextItem>` instances (lines 329 and 345) contain only text content, not icon elements. No icon-to-prop migration is applicable.

### Fix 5 — `TextVariants` → `ContentVariants`
**Applied.** Changed import from `TextVariants` to `ContentVariants` (line 31). Updated JSX usage at line 302 from `TextVariants.small` to `ContentVariants.small`.

### Fix 6 — `Text` → `Content`
**Applied.** Removed `Text` from imports (line 30), added `Content` import. Replaced `<Text component={...}>...</Text>` with content merged into the parent `<Content>` element (lines 302-306).

### Fix 7 — `TextContent` → `Content`
**Applied.** Removed `TextContent` from imports (line 31). Replaced `<TextContent className="...">` wrapper with `<Content className="..." component={ContentVariants.small}>`, merging the wrapper and inner `<Text>` into a single `<Content>` element (lines 302-306).

### Test file
**No change needed.** The companion test file (`create-dr-policy.spec.tsx`) is entirely `describe.skip`-ed. Even if tests ran, none of the queries (`getByText`, `getByTestId`) are affected by the changes — text content is unchanged and no structural selectors were impacted.
